summary: Joel Spolsky briefly muses that a viable business model might be to take 37signals' deliberately simple products and create more complex, feature-rich versions of them. The post is essentially a teaser for a conference talk he gave at the Business of Software conference, where he presumably explored the tension between simplicity and feature complexity in more depth. He also promotes his upcoming appearance at the next year's conference.
key_points: There is a market tension between the simplicity 37signals champions and users who want more features and complexity
Copying a simple product and adding complexity could be a viable business strategy
The simplicity-vs-choice debate is significant enough to warrant a full conference talk
money_quotes: Sometimes I think a pretty good business model would be to copy the applications that 37signals makes, but make them more complex.
key_insight: The deliberate simplicity that companies like 37signals champion leaves a market opening for competitors willing to offer the complexity and features that many users actually want.
